title: Using ${TARGETARCH} in a FROM stage does not work
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

`${TARGETARCH}` is not populated when used in a `FROM` stage.

As documented here: https://docs.docker.com/engine/reference/builder/#automatic-platform-args-in-the-global-scope

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

Given this trimmed down Dockerfile:
```Dockerfile
FROM --platform=$TARGETPLATFORM alpine:3.16.0 AS build

ARG TARGETARCH
ARG TARGETPLATFORM

LABEL architecture=$TARGETARCH

FROM --platform=linux/amd64 build AS platform-amd64
ENV SERVER_JRE_CHECKSUM=297f58d7ff54b4d5936b25c472a701f629fe12d061febcf24506b1c90572ec9e

FROM --platform=linux/arm64 build AS platform-arm64
ENV SERVER_JRE_CHECKSUM=80b62217e00bbadbb03e294937ab88e53375314eb25a3af793a4353158eac6a4

FROM platform-${TARGETARCH} AS final
RUN echo ${SERVER_JRE_CHECKSUM}
```

**Describe the results you received:**
When building with podman (Mac M1), the following error is received:

```shell
# podman build --platform linux/amd64 -t yapstone/java11:test .
time="2022-05-26T16:38:20+10:00" level=warning msg="missing \"TARGETARCH\" build argument. Try adding \"--build-arg TARGETARCH=<VALUE>\" to the command line"
time="2022-05-26T16:38:20+10:00" level=warning msg="missing \"TARGETPLATFORM\" build argument. Try adding \"--build-arg TARGETPLATFORM=<VALUE>\" to the command line"
[1/4] STEP 1/4: FROM alpine:3.16.0 AS build
Resolved "alpine" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/alpine:3.16.0...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:2408cc74d12b6cd092bb8b516ba7d5e290f485d3eb9672efc00f0583730179e8
Copying blob sha256:2408cc74d12b6cd092bb8b516ba7d5e290f485d3eb9672efc00f0583730179e8
Copying config sha256:e66264b98777e12192600bf9b4d663655c98a090072e1bab49e233d7531d1294
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
[1/4] STEP 2/4: ARG TARGETARCH
--> 583290dec75
[1/4] STEP 3/4: ARG TARGETPLATFORM
--> f7ee254aedf
[1/4] STEP 4/4: LABEL architecture=$TARGETARCH
--> f4b9975b2c8
[4/4] STEP 1/2: FROM platform- AS final
Error: error creating build container: invalid reference format
```

You can see that in the final step, `${TARGETARCH}` was interpolated as an empty string.

```shell
[4/4] STEP 1/2: FROM platform- AS final
Error: error creating build container: invalid reference format
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
When building with Docker for Desktop, this builds without issues.

```shell
# docker build --platform linux/amd64 -t test:test .
[+] Building 4.8s (6/6) FINISHED                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     
 => [internal] load build definition from Dockerfile                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            0.0s
 => => transferring dockerfile: 523B                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            0.0s
 => [internal] load .dockerignore                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               0.0s
 => => transferring context: 2B                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 0.0s
 => [internal] load metadata for docker.io/library/alpine:3.16.0                                                                                                                                                                                                                                3.6s
 => [build 1/1] FROM docker.io/library/alpine:3.16.0@sha256:686d8c9dfa6f3ccfc8230bc3178d23f84eeaf7e457f36f271ab1acc53015037c                                                                                                                                                                    0.7s
 => => resolve docker.io/library/alpine:3.16.0@sha256:686d8c9dfa6f3ccfc8230bc3178d23f84eeaf7e457f36f271ab1acc53015037c                                                                                                                                                                          0.0s
 => => sha256:686d8c9dfa6f3ccfc8230bc3178d23f84eeaf7e457f36f271ab1acc53015037c 1.64kB / 1.64kB                                                                                                                                                                                                  0.0s
 => => sha256:4ff3ca91275773af45cb4b0834e12b7eb47d1c18f770a0b151381cd227f4c253 528B / 528B                                                                                                                                                                                                      0.0s
 => => sha256:e66264b98777e12192600bf9b4d663655c98a090072e1bab49e233d7531d1294 1.47kB / 1.47kB                                                                                                                                                                                                  0.0s
 => => sha256:2408cc74d12b6cd092bb8b516ba7d5e290f485d3eb9672efc00f0583730179e8 2.80MB / 2.80MB                                                                                                                                                                                                  0.5s
 => => extracting sha256:2408cc74d12b6cd092bb8b516ba7d5e290f485d3eb9672efc00f0583730179e8                                                                                                                                                                                                       0.2s
 => [final 1/1] RUN echo 297f58d7ff54b4d5936b25c472a701f629fe12d061febcf24506b1c90572ec9e                                                                                                                                                                                                       0.2s
 => exporting to image                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          0.0s
 => => exporting layers                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         0.0s
 => => writing image sha256:5df1e9eec49b48a1d35e50aaf1758efec7983395185ed482158b15eda20bca66                                                                                                                                                                                                    0.0s
 => => naming to docker.io/library/test:test                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    0.0s
Use 'docker scan' to run Snyk tests against images to find vulnerabilities and learn how to fix them
```

You can see it got to the final stage and echoed the right checksum:

```shell
 => [final 1/1] RUN echo 297f58d7ff54b4d5936b25c472a701f629fe12d061febcf24506b1c90572ec9e     
```

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
Always reproducible.

**Output of `podman version`:**

```shell
# podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.0
API Version:  4.1.0
Go Version:   go1.18.1

Built:        Sat May  7 02:16:38 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/arm64

```

Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
N/A

comments.body: Hi @aaronjwhiteside , Thanks for creating the issue. Above PR https://github.com/openshift/imagebuilder/pull/229 should close this but we will have to wait till this gets into buildah and buildah gets into podman.

Wow! Thanks @flouthoc for the super quick turn around time!

Do you have any rough kind of estimate when this gets picked up by buildah and then finally podman, are we talking days, weeks or months?
It should be in podman 4.2 and buildah 1.27.0,  I would figure July/August time frame.

Hi @aaronjwhiteside , I am closing this issue since imagebuilder PR is already merged and following PR will sync support in buildah https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/4061

Please feei free to continue discussion below.

Thanks
